terms.definition: Tall, carved stone shafts erected as monuments. In Aksum, they marked royal tombs and are famous for mimicking timber-frame buildings in stone.
Seasonal winds in the Indian Ocean that reverse direction twice a year. Ancient sailors used them to travel between India and Africa, making the Red Sea trade network possible.
A 1st-century AD Greek sailing guide (periplus) written by an anonymous merchant. It is our most important primary source for the early trade of the Red Sea and Indian Ocean.
Meaning 'Our Father,' the title of the head of the Ethiopian Church. Historically an Egyptian monk appointed by the Patriarch of Alexandria.
The world's oldest surviving illuminated Gospels, kept at the Abba Garima Monastery. Their existence confirms the high level of culture in Aksum during the time of the Nine Saints.
The migration of early Muslims to Aksum (c. 615 AD) to escape persecution in Mecca. The Aksumite king's refusal to hand them over is a key event in Islamic-Christian relations.
The ancient Jewish community of Ethiopia, which practiced a pre-Talmudic form of Judaism.
